title: WolfHsmError::check inlines two-level triage with zero-cost func name
description: error.rs: WolfHsmError::check takes an i32 return code and a &'static str function name, then routes to Wh{code} for the WH_ERROR_* range or Ffi{code,func} for everything else. The &'static str is zero-cost at runtime (it is a literal in the binary) and eliminates the need to grep headers to identify which C call failed. A future contributor might suggest removing func from Ffi to simplify the enum, or replacing it with a String to allow runtime-constructed names. Both would be regressions: removing func makes production error messages unactionable without reattaching a debugger; using String adds allocation to every failed FFI call on the hot path and loses the compile-time guarantee that the name is a string literal. The current shape — a single inline fn that is called immediately after every unsafe block — is also the pattern that makes auditing unsafe code tractable.
